channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Mixed verdict for 3 Memphis officers convicted in Tyre Nichols' fatal beating
item.description: MEMPHIS, Tenn. (AP) — Three former Memphis police officers were convicted Thursday in the 2023 fatal beating of Tyre Nichols, but were acquitted of the harshest charges they faced for a death that sparked national protests and calls for broad changes in policing.
item.dc:creator: ADRIAN SAINZ, KRISTIN M. HALL and JONATHAN MATTISE, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Los Angeles prosecutors to review new evidence in Menendez brothers' 1996 murder conviction
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — Prosecutors in Los Angeles are reviewing new evidence in the case of Erik and Lyle Menendez to determine whether they should be serving life sentences for killing their parents in their Beverly Hills mansion more than 35 years ago, the city's district attorney said Thursday.
item.dc:creator: CHRISTOPHER WEBER and JAIMIE DING, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Nibi the 'diva' beaver to stay at rescue center, Massachusetts governor decides
item.description: BOSTON (AP) — The question of whether a 2-year-old beaver named Nibi can stay with the rescuers she has known since she was a baby or must be released into the wild was resolved Thursday when the Massachusetts governor stepped in to protect Nibi.
item.dc:creator: STEVE LeBLANC, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Rhode Island woman works 350 hours to build first home
item.description: "It makes you excited to think that you're going to build your own home and help others to get theirs," she told NewsNatoin affiliate WPRI. "That's like a dream come true.
item.dc:creator: Catie McNeil, NewsNation

item.title: Texas couple raffling ACL tickets to pay for costly endometriosis surgery
item.description: Cole Stepro first noticed symptoms in December 2023 and started a 10-month journey with 17 doctors across the country.
item.dc:creator: Taylor Girtman, NewsNation

item.title: The Latest: Harris campaigns in Wisconsin and Trump in Michigan in battle for 'blue wall' states
item.description: Democratic presidential candidate Kamala Harris is opening a two-day stay in Wisconsin and Michigan, and Republican candidate Donald Trump will be in Michigan on Thursday as the two candidates grapple for wins in the “blue wall” battleground states, which also include Pennsylvania.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Dockworkers' union to suspend strike until Jan. 15
item.description: The union representing 45,000 striking U.S. dockworkers at East and Gulf coast ports reached a deal Thursday to suspend a three-day strike until Jan. 15 to provide time to negotiate a new contract.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Dockworkers' union to suspend strike until Jan. 15 to allow time to negotiate new contract
item.description: The union representing 45,000 striking U.S. dockworkers at East and Gulf coast ports has reached a deal to suspend their strike until Jan. 15 to provide time to negotiate a new contract, a person briefed on the matter says.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: 3 officers convicted in Tyre Nichols fatal beating, 2 acquitted of civil rights charges
item.description: Three former Memphis officers were convicted Thursday of charges of witness tampering in the 2023 fatal beating of Tyre Nichols, and two were acquitted of federal civil rights violations in a death that sparked national protests and calls for broad changes in policing.
item.dc:creator: Megan Fayard, WGN 9

item.title: Los Angeles prosecutors to review new evidence in Menendez brothers' 1996 murder conviction
item.description: Prosecutors in Los Angeles say they are reviewing new evidence in the case of the Menendez brothers to determine whether they should be serving life sentences for killing their parents in their Beverly Hills mansion more than 35 years ago.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Country music star Garth Brooks accused of sexual assault by makeup artist
item.description: Garth Brooks, the country music superstar who gained fame in the 1990s with several hit songs, has been named in a lawsuit accusing him of raping a hair and makeup artist in 2019.
item.dc:creator: Austin Turner, KRON4

item.title: Pennsylvania US Senate: Bob Casey, Dave McCormick trade jabs in debate
item.description: The candidates battled throughout the debate, often claiming the other was lying on the campaign trail and on the debate stage.
item.dc:creator: George Stockburger, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Texas teen abducted from Dallas Mavericks NBA game shares what lured her from dad
item.description: Natalee Cramer, now 18, is speaking out for the first time since she was abducted from a Dallas Mavericks game and allegedly forced into sex trafficking in 2022.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Blue Ridge Parkway to be closed indefinitely, National Park Service says
item.description: It comes right as the region – and road – typically sees a boom in usage as weather cools and the leaves change colors.
item.dc:creator: Doug Coats, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Federal agencies warn of scams in wake of Helene
item.description: “These criminals take advantage of victims before, during, and after a natural disaster strikes, targeting people when they are most vulnerable, and their methods to commit fraud are wide-ranging,” one U.S. Attorney said.
item.dc:creator: Tara Suter, KRON4

item.title: Hurricane Helene renews calls for Congress to pass disaster funding
item.description: The widespread devastation wrought by Hurricane Helene is upping the pressure on lawmakers for a looming end-of-year fight over disaster funding.
item.dc:creator: Rachel Frazin, NewsNation

item.title: Melania Trump says she supports abortion rights, putting her at odds with the GOP
item.description: Melania Trump has revealed her support for abortion rights ahead of the release of her upcoming memoir.
item.dc:creator: Christine Fernando, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Biden student loan forgiveness plan gets win in Georgia court
item.description: President Joe Biden’s student debt relief plan was dealt a win by a Georgia judge this week by removing one Republican challenger and letting the program advance for now after a temporarily restraining order expires.
item.dc:creator: Lexi Lonas Cochran, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Florida law banning homeless people from sleeping in public spaces takes effect
item.description: Under the law, municipalities are required to create designated spaces for camping and sleeping if homeless shelters are full.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Voters in 13 states have less than a week to register. When is your deadline?
item.description: The 2024 presidential election is fast approaching, and in some states, the voter registration deadline is as soon as this week.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Jackson, Fox 5

item.title: Wawa named No. 1 convenience store in consumer satisfaction survey
item.description: Analysts noted that many of the top-ranked chains specialized in ready-to-eat foods prepared in the stores' kitchens.
item.dc:creator: Jessica Yakubovsky, KRON4

item.title: US school-entry vaccination rates fall as exemptions keep rising
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — U.S. kindergarten vaccination rates dipped last year and the proportion of children with exemptions rose to an all-time high, according to federal data posted Wednesday. The share of kids exempted from vaccine requirements rose to 3.3%, up from 3% the year before.
item.dc:creator: MIKE STOBBE, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Tropical Storm Leslie forms; Hurricane Kirk strengthens
item.description: Hurricane Kirk is expected to grow larger as it strengthens in the Atlantic, the National Hurricane Center said.
item.dc:creator: Ashley Suter, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Florida enacts tough law to get homeless off the streets, leaving cities and counties scrambling
item.description: FORT LAUDERDALE, Fla. (AP) — As the sun rose Tuesday, Robert Robinson pulled himself from the sidewalk outside Fort Lauderdale's bus depot. It's where he'd slept — and become a violator of Florida's newest law.
item.dc:creator: TERRY SPENCER and KATE PAYNE, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: My car was submerged in floodwaters. Should I start it?
item.description: Storm surges and flooding from Hurricane Helene have caused widespread devastation across parts of the Southeastern U.S., in some cases leaving homes and cars completely underwater.
item.dc:creator: Michael Bartiromo, Fox 5

item.title: Helene and other storms dumped a whopping 40 trillion gallons of rain on the South
item.description: More than 40 trillion gallons of rain drenched the Southeast United States in the last week from Hurricane Helene and a run-of-the-mill rainstorm that sloshed in ahead of it — an unheard of amount of water that has stunned experts.
item.dc:creator: SETH BORENSTEIN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Helene's death toll surpasses 150 as crews continue to search for survivors
item.description: Exhausted emergency workers continued to work around-the-clock to clear roads, restore power and cellphone service, and reach people left stranded by Hurricane Helene.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Justice Department will launch civil rights review into 1921 Tulsa Race Massacre
item.description: OKLAHOMA CITY (AP) — The Justice Department announced Monday it plans to launch a review of the 1921 Tulsa Race Massacre, an attack by a white mob on a thriving Black district that is considered one of the worst single acts of violence against Black people in U.S. history.
item.dc:creator: SEAN MURPHY, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Favre tries to expand his defamation lawsuit against Mississippi auditor over welfare spending
item.description: Favre is not facing criminal charges, but he is among more than three dozen people or businesses the state is suing to try to recover misspent money through the Temporary Assistance for Needy Families program.
item.dc:creator: EMILY WAGSTER PETTUS, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: NYC Mayor Eric Adams accepted harmless 'courtesies,' not bribes, his lawyer says
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — New York City Mayor Eric Adams launched a legal attack on the federal corruption case against him Monday, with his attorney asking a judge to toss out bribery charges and then holding a combative news conference accusing prosecutors of ethical lapses.
item.dc:creator: JAKE OFFENHARTZ, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: iPhone stuck in SOS mode? Here's how to get out of it
item.description: There are ongoing reports of a widespread outage impacting multiple cellphone and internet providers Monday morning. According to Downdetector.com, AT&T, Verizon and T-Mobile users have all experienced issues.
item.dc:creator: Alex Baker, ABC 4

item.title: Utah school district makes list of 50 best districts in America for 2025: Report
item.description: The rankings are based on ratings from current students, alumni, and parents, along with quantitative data from the U.S. Department of Education — and this district in Utah made the list.
item.dc:creator: Jeremy Tanner, ABC 4

item.title: Submerged EVs can catch fire when inundated with saltwater: What to know
item.description: Electric vehicles can catch fire if they are inundated by saltwater, so owners who live in the path of a major storm should take precautions and prepare for the possibility that they'll be unable to charge their cars during a power outage.
item.dc:creator: Josh Funk, Fox 5

item.title: A century of Jimmy Carter: Longest-living former president turns 100
item.description: In 1998, at the ripe age of 74, former President Carter put some of his thoughts about getting older to paper. "The virtues of aging," Carter wrote in a book that year, "include both the blessings that come to us as we grow older and what we have to offer that might be beneficial to others.
item.dc:creator: Judy Kurtz, WGN 9

item.title: Man is sentenced to 35 years for shooting 2 Jewish men as they left Los Angeles synagogues
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — A California man was sentenced Monday to 35 years in prison for shooting and wounding two Jewish men as they left synagogues in Los Angeles last year, federal prosecutors said.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: When do hurricane names get retired?
item.description: With nearly two months to go until the end of what was predicted to be a "hyperactive" hurricane season, many residents along Florida's Gulf Coast and southeastern states are grappling with the damage and destruction left in the wake of the latest hurricane.
item.dc:creator: Kaycee Sloan, Fox 5

item.title: Donate today to help Red Cross efforts across 10 states
item.description: Days after Hurricane Helene's deadly force tore a path north from the Gulf Coast, many Americans remained without power, water and other basic necessities.
item.dc:creator: Bill Disbrow, Fox 5
